rules.title: MOVEMENT
rules.overview: To move a Model, measure the intended distance from its Base. Then, move the Model, ensuring it doesn’t exceed the measured distance or pass through enemy Models or Terrain.

subsections.title: OBSTACLES
subsections.body: Terrain that is 1” tall or shorter is called an Obstacle. An Infantry Model may Move over an Obstacle by reducing their total Movement by 2”.

subsections.title: GOING UP OR DOWN
subsections.body: Infantry Models can Move up or down using things like stairs or ladders. The Model must end its Movement with their Base fully flat on a surface.

subsections.title: LEANING OUT
subsections.body: A Model may use half its Movement to Lean Out after a Move. If a Model only Leans Out instead of Moving, it does not count as Moving for Special Rules. Place a 25mm Lean Out Marker next to the Model’s Base. The Marker can be used for the Model’s Line of Sight and measurements. Enemy Models must treat the Marker as the Leaning Out Model if they do not have Line of Sight to the Model itself. Models that are Leaning Out have cover from any Model that can only see the Marker and not the actual Model. Remove the Marker before the Model moves. LEANING OUT • Model B chooses to Lean Out instead of Moving. • A Lean Out Marker is placed next to the Models Base. • Model A only has Line of Sight to the Lean Out Marker and not Model B. Model B will have Cover from Model A.

subsections.title: JUMP (X)
subsections.body: Models with Jump(X) can Jump as a Movement. The Model may be placed anywhere within (X)” horizontally, and (X)” vertically without passing through Terrain.

rules.body: To move a Model, measure the intended distance from its Base. Then, move the Model, ensuring it doesn’t exceed the measured distance or pass through enemy Models or Terrain. [2.1] OBSTACLES Terrain that is 1” tall or shorter is called an Obstacle. An Infantry Model may Move over an Obstacle by reducing their total Movement by 2”. [2.2] GOING UP OR DOWN Infantry Models can Move up or down using things like stairs or ladders. The Model must end its Movement with their Base fully flat on a surface. [2.3] LEANING OUT A Model may use half its Movement to Lean Out after a Move. If a Model only Leans Out instead of Moving, it does not count as Moving for Special Rules. Place a 25mm Lean Out Marker next to the Model’s Base. The Marker can be used for the Model’s Line of Sight and measurements. Enemy Models must treat the Marker as the Leaning Out Model if they do not have Line of Sight to the Model itself. Models that are Leaning Out have cover from any Model that can only see the Marker and not the actual Model. Remove the Marker before the Model moves. LEANING OUT • Model B chooses to Lean Out instead of Moving. • A Lean Out Marker is placed next to the Models Base. • Model A only has Line of Sight to the Lean Out Marker and not Model B. Model B will have Cover from Model A. [2.4] JUMP (X) Models with Jump(X) can Jump as a Movement. The Model may be placed anywhere within (X)” horizontally, and (X)” vertically without passing through Terrain.

rules.title: COMBAT

subsections.title: SHOOTING STEPS
subsections.body: 1. Pick a Model that your Shooting Model has Line of Sight to and is within its Weapon’s Range. 1. Confirm if the Target Model is in Cover. 1. Roll a Skill Check (2D10), rolling an additional D10 if the Target is not in Cover and subtracting a D10 if the Target is in Cover. (D10 in a Shooting Skill Check are called Shots.) 1. For each Success, the Target suffers Damage equal to the Weapon’s Damage. (Yes! Shots that Ace by rolling a 10 apply double the Weapons Damage!) 1. The Target must make an Armor Check, rolling a number of D10 equal to the first number in their Armor, and preventing a point of Damage for each D10 that rolls equal to or higher than the second number. ARMOR If a Model suffers more Damage than it has Armor (D10), it cannot stop that Damage and must mark it.

subsections.title: MARKING DAMAGE
subsections.body: When a Model fails to prevent Damage, it must mark a number of Damage on its Card equal to the Damage it suffered. When the Damage Track is filled, the Model is Destroyed and removed from the Game. Models without a Damage Track are Destroyed when they suffer at least one Damage.

subsections.title: CQC
subsections.body: After making an Action, an Infantry Model in Base-to-Base with an Enemy Infantry Model will engage in Close Quarters Combat (CQC). Both Models will make a Skill Check. Each Success inflicts one Damage to the other Model in CQC, ignoring Armor Checks. The Model with more Successes applies their Damage first. If this would Destroy the other Model, the winning Model does not suffer any Damage. If Players roll an equal number of Successes, the Player with the highest roll on a D10 wins; If Players roll the same number, they will roll again. When a Model is Destroyed in CQC, the CQC ends, and Players proceed to the next CQC.

subsections.title: EXPLOSIVES
subsections.body: Explosive Weapons, like grenades, include the Blast(X) Special Rule. Explosive Weapons may not be used to Target Infantry Models. Instead they will follow these rules: HOW TO SHOOT EXPLOSIVES: 1. Place a Blast Marker (25mm) where your Model has Line of Sight to it. This Marker must be flat and stable on the Table or Terrain, so it shouldn’t be hanging off an edge or floating. 2. After placing the Blast Marker the Shooting Model must make a Skill Check. 3. Infantry within X” of the Mark must make an Armor Check, rolling a number of D10 equal to the number of Successes the Shooting Model made. Models must mark a point of Damage for each D10 that rolls under their Armor. Damage caused by an Explosive Weapon is considered Blast Damage. THROWING GRENADES THROUGH WINDOWS? Players may agree that it would make sense for a Model to place the Marker for an Explosive Weapon outside of a Models Line of Sight.

rules.body: [3.1] SHOOTING STEPS 1. Pick a Model that your Shooting Model has Line of Sight to and is within its Weapon’s Range. 1. Confirm if the Target Model is in Cover. 1. Roll a Skill Check (2D10), rolling an additional D10 if the Target is not in Cover and subtracting a D10 if the Target is in Cover. (D10 in a Shooting Skill Check are called Shots.) 1. The Target must make an Armor Check, rolling a number of D10 equal to the first number in their Armor, and preventing a point of Damage for each D10 that rolls equal to or higher than the second number. ARMOR If a Model suffers more Damage than it has Armor (D10), it cannot stop that Damage and must mark it. [3.2] MARKING DAMAGE When a Model fails to prevent Damage, it must mark a number of Damage on its Card equal to the Damage it suffered. When the Damage Track is filled, the Model is Destroyed and removed from the Game. Models without a Damage Track are Destroyed when they suffer at least one Damage. [3.3] CQC After making an Action, an Infantry Model in Base-to-Base with an Enemy Infantry Model will engage in Close Quarters Combat (CQC). Both Models will make a Skill Check. Each Success inflicts one Damage to the other Model in CQC, ignoring Armor Checks. The Model with more Successes applies their Damage first. If this would Destroy the other Model, the winning Model does not suffer any Damage. If Players roll an equal number of Successes, the Player with the highest roll on a D10 wins; If Players roll the same number, they will roll again. When a Model is Destroyed in CQC, the CQC ends, and Players proceed to the next CQC. [3.4] EXPLOSIVES Explosive Weapons, like grenades, include the Blast(X) Special Rule. Explosive Weapons may not be used to Target Infantry Models. Instead they will follow these rules: HOW TO SHOOT EXPLOSIVES: 1. Place a Blast Marker (25mm) where your Model has Line of Sight to it. This Marker must be flat and stable on the Table or Terrain, so it shouldn’t be hanging off an edge or floating. 2. After placing the Blast Marker the Shooting Model must make a Skill Check. 3. Infantry within X” of the Mark must make an Armor Check, rolling a number of D10 equal to the number of Successes the Shooting Model made. Models must mark a point of Damage for each D10 that rolls under their Armor. Damage caused by an Explosive Weapon is considered Blast Damage. THROWING GRENADES THROUGH WINDOWS? Players may agree that it would make sense for a Model to place the Marker for an Explosive Weapon outside of a Models Line of Sight.

rules.title: GAME ROUNDS
rules.overview: In BLKOUT, a game is made of Rounds, each with two parts: Operations and Execution. Players follow these steps in order, starting with the Operations.

subsections.title: OPERATIONS
subsections.body: 1. Pick Up: Players will pick up all Activation Markers, Ready Tokens, and Engaged Tokens from their Models and Units. 2. Determine Initiative: To determine who will go first in a Round, both Players will roll a D10, rerolling if they roll the same number. The Player that rolls the higher number will Activate a Unit in the Execution first.

subsections.title: EXECUTION
subsections.body: 1. Activations: Players will take turns Activating Units, starting with the Player who won the Initiative. 2. Ending a Round: The Execution ends once both Players have Activated all of their Units. After the Execution ends, a new Round will begin. [6.0] ACTIVATING A UNIT Activating a Unit allows a Player to Move their Models and Destroy the Enemy! • Units with an Activation Marker cannot Activate. • Units with no remaining Models cannot Activate. Follow these steps to Activate a Unit: 1. Reposition: During the Reposition step every Model in the Unit may Move. 2. Actions: Once every Model has Moved, each Model must make one Action each. Resolve them one at a time. ENGAGED! Models with an Engaged Token cannot make an Action. [6.1] ACTIONS Shoot: The Model may Shoot a target. [3.1] Ready: The Model gets a Ready Token. (Ready Tokens allow the Model to make a Reaction [7.0]) Sprint: The Model may Move. When making Actions, Enemy Models may React! Reactions are covered under [7.0]. 1. Activation Marker: After all Active Models have finished their Actions, place an Activation Marker on the Unit to show it has Activated for this Round.

rules.body: In BLKOUT, a game is made of Rounds, each with two parts: Operations and Execution. Players follow these steps in order, starting with the Operations. [5.1] OPERATIONS 1. Pick Up: Players will pick up all Activation Markers, Ready Tokens, and Engaged Tokens from their Models and Units. 2. Determine Initiative: To determine who will go first in a Round, both Players will roll a D10, rerolling if they roll the same number. The Player that rolls the higher number will Activate a Unit in the Execution first. [5.3] EXECUTION 1. Activations: Players will take turns Activating Units, starting with the Player who won the Initiative. 2. Ending a Round: The Execution ends once both Players have Activated all of their Units. After the Execution ends, a new Round will begin. [6.0] ACTIVATING A UNIT Activating a Unit allows a Player to Move their Models and Destroy the Enemy! • Units with an Activation Marker cannot Activate. • Units with no remaining Models cannot Activate. Follow these steps to Activate a Unit: 1. Reposition: During the Reposition step every Model in the Unit may Move. 2. Actions: Once every Model has Moved, each Model must make one Action each. Resolve them one at a time. ENGAGED! Models with an Engaged Token cannot make an Action. [6.1] ACTIONS Shoot: The Model may Shoot a target. [3.1] Ready: The Model gets a Ready Token. (Ready Tokens allow the Model to make a Reaction [7.0]) Sprint: The Model may Move. When making Actions, Enemy Models may React! Reactions are covered under [7.0]. 1. Activation Marker: After all Active Models have finished their Actions, place an Activation Marker on the Unit to show it has Activated for this Round.

rules.title: REACTIONS

subsections.title: MAKING REACTIONS
subsections.body: A Model in a Unit that has not Activated yet and Models with a Ready Token may make Reactions. When Models make a Reaction, they gain an Engaged Token, replacing any Ready Tokens they have. Models with an Engaged Token cannot make Reactions or Actions.

subsections.title: RETURN FIRE REACTION
subsections.body: A single Model may Return Fire and Shoot an Enemy Model when it makes a Shooting Action. Both Models will roll their Skill Check at the same time, with the Model that rolls more Successes applying their Damage first. If a Model is Destroyed, its Shooting will not apply Damage. If Players roll an equal number of Successes, the Player that rolls the highest number on a D10 will win. If Players roll the same number, they will roll again.

subsections.title: OVERWATCH REACTION
subsections.body: Any Model may make the Overwatch Reaction to Shoot an Enemy Model when it ends an Action, or ends a Movement that placed it farther than half its Movement Value in their Line of Sight. EXAMPLE A Model with a Movement Value of 4” Moves 3”. Enemies could React with Overwatch to this Movement since it is farther than half the Models Movement Value. REMEMBER! A D10 that rolls a 10 in a Skill Check is an Ace and counts as two Successes! COMBAT EXAMPLE 1. Model A begins a Shoot Action Targeting Model B. 2. Model C gains an Engaged Token to make the Return Fire Reaction and Shoot Model A. 1. Model A and C roll their Skill Check at the same time. 2. Model A rolls two Successes, while Model C rolls one. Since Model A rolled more Successes than Model C, they apply their Damage to Model B first. 3. Model B only has Armor 1/6, so it can only prevent one point of Damage total. With two Damage, it will be Destroyed. 1. After Destroying Model B, Model C’s Success now hits Model A. Causing them to make an Armor Check due to the one Damage. They have Armor 1/6 so must roll 1D10, being Destroyed on a five or less.

subsections.title: JUKE REACTION
subsections.body: When Targeted by Shooting, an Infantry Model may gain an Engaged Token to gain Cover from the Shooting Model.

rules.body: [7.1] MAKING REACTIONS A Model in a Unit that has not Activated yet and Models with a Ready Token may make Reactions. When Models make a Reaction, they gain an Engaged Token, replacing any Ready Tokens they have. Models with an Engaged Token cannot make Reactions or Actions. [7.2] RETURN FIRE REACTION A single Model may Return Fire and Shoot an Enemy Model when it makes a Shooting Action. Both Models will roll their Skill Check at the same time, with the Model that rolls more Successes applying their Damage first. If a Model is Destroyed, its Shooting will not apply Damage. If Players roll an equal number of Successes, the Player that rolls the highest number on a D10 will win. If Players roll the same number, they will roll again. [7.3] OVERWATCH REACTION Any Model may make the Overwatch Reaction to Shoot an Enemy Model when it ends an Action, or ends a Movement that placed it farther than half its Movement Value in their Line of Sight. EXAMPLE A Model with a Movement Value of 4” Moves 3”. Enemies could React with Overwatch to this Movement since it is farther than half the Models Movement Value. REMEMBER! A D10 that rolls a 10 in a Skill Check is an Ace and counts as two Successes! COMBAT EXAMPLE 1. Model A begins a Shoot Action Targeting Model B. 2. Model C gains an Engaged Token to make the Return Fire Reaction and Shoot Model A. 1. Model A and C roll their Skill Check at the same time. 2. Model A rolls two Successes, while Model C rolls one. Since Model A rolled more Successes than Model C, they apply their Damage to Model B first. 3. Model B only has Armor 1/6, so it can only prevent one point of Damage total. With two Damage, it will be Destroyed. 1. After Destroying Model B, Model C’s Success now hits Model A. Causing them to make an Armor Check due to the one Damage. They have Armor 1/6 so must roll 1D10, being Destroyed on a five or less. [7.4] JUKE REACTION When Targeted by Shooting, an Infantry Model may gain an Engaged Token to gain Cover from the Shooting Model.

rules.title: DATA ATTACKS
rules.overview: Models equipped with a Data Spike can make a Data Attack Action. This Action requires a Skill Check. If Successful, the Player has the choice to either: Give an Enemy Unit on the Table a Pinned Marker. Remove a Pinned Marker from a Unit. PINNED MARKER A Unit with a Pinned Marker must remove their Pinned Marker instead of Moving any of their Models during the Reposition Step.
rules.body: Models equipped with a Data Spike can make a Data Attack Action. This Action requires a Skill Check. If Successful, the Player has the choice to either: Give an Enemy Unit on the Table a Pinned Marker. Remove a Pinned Marker from a Unit. PINNED MARKER A Unit with a Pinned Marker must remove their Pinned Marker instead of Moving any of their Models during the Reposition Step.

rules.title: CONTROL POINTS
rules.overview: Before each Game, Players will gain three Control Points. They may spend one Control Point to: 1. Roll an additional D10 during Initiative. 2. Allow a Unit to benefit from a Battle Drill during its Activation. 3. Activate another Unit after finishing an Activation.
rules.body: Before each Game, Players will gain three Control Points. They may spend one Control Point to: 1. Roll an additional D10 during Initiative. 2. Allow a Unit to benefit from a Battle Drill during its Activation. 3. Activate another Unit after finishing an Activation.

rules.title: SMOKE
rules.overview: Smoke is represented by a Smoke, or a Light Smoke Token. Line of Sight and Smoke: Models cannot draw Line of Sight through the area within 3” of the Smoke Token. Models within 3” of the Smoke Token are considered in Cover and treat their Targets as in Cover. Smoke Dissipation: Before determining Initiative, a Player rolls one D10 for each Smoke Token on the Table. On a roll of 6 or higher, the respective Smoke Token is removed from the Game. Light Smoke: If the Smoke Token is not removed, the Player who originally placed it must replace it with a Light Smoke Token. This Light Smoke Token may be placed within 1” of the original Smoke Token’s location before the Smoke Token is removed. All Light Smoke Tokens are removed from the Game before determining Initiative with no roll required.

All Light Smoke Tokens are removed from the Game before determining Initiative with no roll required.

effectiveRules.effectiveText: Core: [7.1] MAKING REACTIONS A Model in a Unit that has not Activated yet and Models with a Ready Token may make Reactions. When Models make a Reaction, they gain an Engaged Token, replacing any Ready Tokens they have. Models with an Engaged Token cannot make Reactions or Actions. [7.2] RETURN FIRE REACTION A single Model may Return Fire and Shoot an Enemy Model when it makes a Shooting Action. Both Models will roll their Skill Check at the same time, with the Model that rolls more Successes applying their Damage first. If a Model is Destroyed, its Shooting will not apply Damage. If Players roll an equal number of Successes, the Player that rolls the highest number on a D10 will win. If Players roll the same number, they will roll again. [7.3] OVERWATCH REACTION Any Model may make the Overwatch Reaction to Shoot an Enemy Model when it ends an Action, or ends a Movement that placed it farther than half its Movement Value in their Line of Sight. EXAMPLE A Model with a Movement Value of 4” Moves 3”. Enemies could React with Overwatch to this Movement since it is farther than half the Models Movement Value. REMEMBER! A D10 that rolls a 10 in a Skill Check is an Ace and counts as two Successes! COMBAT EXAMPLE 1. Model A begins a Shoot Action Targeting Model B. 2. Model C gains an Engaged Token to make the Return Fire Reaction and Shoot Model A. 1. Model A and C roll their Skill Check at the same time. 2. Model A rolls two Successes, while Model C rolls one. Since Model A rolled more Successes than Model C, they apply their Damage to Model B first. 3. Model B only has Armor 1/6, so it can only prevent one point of Damage total. With two Damage, it will be Destroyed. 1. After Destroying Model B, Model C’s Success now hits Model A. Causing them to make an Armor Check due to the one Damage. They have Armor 1/6 so must roll 1D10, being Destroyed on a five or less. [7.4] JUKE REACTION When Targeted by Shooting, an Infantry Model may gain an Engaged Token to gain Cover from the Shooting Model. Override 1: Q: Can Blast [X] weapons be used in Reactions? A: Only against vehicles and Dusters, as Blast Weapons cannot Target Infantry Models. Q: Can Blast weapons trigger the Return Fire Reaction? A: Yes, as using Blast weapons is still making a Shoot Action. Q: Does Cyclic work on Reaction Shooting? A: No, Cyclic only triggers on a Shoot Action, which can only be done by an active Unit. Q: What happens when you get an Ace on the backside of a duster? Is it double Damage+1 or does it double the bonus Damage as well? A: The weapon gaining +1 Damage occurs before dice are rolled in the Shooting action or Reaction.
effectiveRules.precedenceReason: Supplemental FAQ clarifies timing and eligibility for reaction cases.

effectiveRules.title: DATA ATTACKS
effectiveRules.originalText: Models equipped with a Data Spike can make a Data Attack Action. This Action requires a Skill Check. If Successful, the Player has the choice to either: Give an Enemy Unit on the Table a Pinned Marker. Remove a Pinned Marker from a Unit. PINNED MARKER A Unit with a Pinned Marker must remove their Pinned Marker instead of Moving any of their Models during the Reposition Step.
effectiveRules.effectiveText: Core: Models equipped with a Data Spike can make a Data Attack Action. This Action requires a Skill Check. If Successful, the Player has the choice to either: Give an Enemy Unit on the Table a Pinned Marker. Remove a Pinned Marker from a Unit. PINNED MARKER A Unit with a Pinned Marker must remove their Pinned Marker instead of Moving any of their Models during the Reposition Step. Override 1: AP - Targeting Dusters and Vehicles (As per normal Shooting rules). • Pick a Duster or Vehicle Model that your Shooting Model has line of sight to, and is within the Weapon’s Range. • Determine Cover. • Make Skill Check (2D10), +1D10 if the Target is not in Cover or Data Attacks don’t require Line of Sight to their Target. -1D10 if they are. Q: Do Data Attacks have a maximum range or require Line of sight? A: If a Range is listed on the Data weapon, that is its maximum range. If no Range is listed, it has unlimited Range. Q: Can Data Knives give Units a Pinned Token? A: No, Data Knives and Data Spikes cause different results when they succeed a Data Attack Action:
effectiveRules.precedenceReason: Supplemental clarifications define data attack range, line-of-sight, and weapon-specific outcomes.

citations.label: AP - Targeting Dusters and Vehicles (As per normal Shooting rules).

effectiveRules.title: CONTROL POINTS
effectiveRules.originalText: Before each Game, Players will gain three Control Points. They may spend one Control Point to: 1. Roll an additional D10 during Initiative. 2. Allow a Unit to benefit from a Battle Drill during its Activation. 3. Activate another Unit after finishing an Activation.
effectiveRules.effectiveText: Core: Before each Game, Players will gain three Control Points. They may spend one Control Point to: 1. Roll an additional D10 during Initiative. 2. Allow a Unit to benefit from a Battle Drill during its Activation. 3. Activate another Unit after finishing an Activation. Override 1: Players will begin each game with 3 Control Points. They may never regain these Control Points. Players may spend a Control Point to: Battle Drills | Spend a Control Point before Activating a Force Unit to allow it to benefit from a Battle Drill during its Activation. A Unit may only benefit from one Battle Drill per Activation. Handler Activation | Once per Round, spend a Control Point to remove an Activation Marker, and any Ready or Engaged Tokens from a Handler after it has finished its Activation. Chained Activation | Once per Round you may spend a Control Point to Activate a Unit after finishing an Activation with a different Unit.
effectiveRules.precedenceReason: Matched play supplemental replaces the core control point economy.

effectiveRules.title: SMOKE
effectiveRules.originalText: Smoke is represented by a Smoke, or a Light Smoke Token. Line of Sight and Smoke: Models cannot draw Line of Sight through the area within 3” of the Smoke Token. Models within 3” of the Smoke Token are considered in Cover and treat their Targets as in Cover. Smoke Dissipation: Before determining Initiative, a Player rolls one D10 for each Smoke Token on the Table. On a roll of 6 or higher, the respective Smoke Token is removed from the Game. Light Smoke: If the Smoke Token is not removed, the Player who originally placed it must replace it with a Light Smoke Token. This Light Smoke Token may be placed within 1” of the original Smoke Token’s location before the Smoke Token is removed. All Light Smoke Tokens are removed from the Game before determining Initiative with no roll required.
effectiveRules.effectiveText: Core: Smoke is represented by a Smoke, or a Light Smoke Token. Line of Sight and Smoke: Models cannot draw Line of Sight through the area within 3” of the Smoke Token. Models within 3” of the Smoke Token are considered in Cover and treat their Targets as in Cover. Smoke Dissipation: Before determining Initiative, a Player rolls one D10 for each Smoke Token on the Table. On a roll of 6 or higher, the respective Smoke Token is removed from the Game. Light Smoke: If the Smoke Token is not removed, the Player who originally placed it must replace it with a Light Smoke Token. This Light Smoke Token may be placed within 1” of the original Smoke Token’s location before the Smoke Token is removed. All Light Smoke Tokens are removed from the Game before determining Initiative with no roll required. Override 1: Models with only part of their base inside Smoke gain Partial Cover and treat their Targets as in Partial Cover. Models with their entire base inside Smoke gain Full Cover and treat their Targets as in Full Cover. Line of Sight may not be drawn through a 3” area around a Smoke Token to Models or Markers outside of the area around the Token.
effectiveRules.precedenceReason: Matched play supplemental replaces core smoke cover behavior when using matched play rules.

faq.question: Does a Unit keep its Armory Item?
faq.answer: No. A Model benefits from the Armory or uses the Weapon only during the Activation in which it marked its Combat Load. Imagine the Armory as limited use or one time abilities.

faq.question: Can I activate more than one Battle Drill on a Unit?
faq.answer: No. You can only choose one Battle Drill for the Unit to benefit from during its Activation.

faq.question: Does the Impisi Force Rule Apply to the whole Force or just the Impisi Insurgents Unit?
faq.answer: The Impisi Force Rule applies to the entire Force, not just the Unit.

faq.question: Is there Unit coherency?
faq.answer: No.

faq.question: Can a Lean Out Move be Reacted to by Overwatch?
faq.answer: Only if the Model is leaning out during a sprint action. The Model is spending its Movement not Moving that distance when Leaning Out.

faq.question: When Jumping do I have to have Line of Sight to the final point?
faq.answer: No, as long as Terrain doesn’t prevent the Model from “moving” from its original point to the new point. IE: Jumping through a wall of a totally enclosed structure.

faq.question: Can a Unit with Jump (4) jump over an obstacle that is 4 inches high and end up on ground level on the other side? (Or would they only be allowed to jump up onto the obstacle?) The Model is allowed to be placed on the other side of the obstacle if there is no intervening Terrain in the way, such as a roof. Can a Model come down from a height if they do not have the Jump (X) rule?
faq.answer: No. The Model must find a safer way down, such as stairs or a ladder.

faq.question: Can a Model climb a surface, or does a Model wishing to move vertically require an obvious means to do so (e.g., ladder, stack of crates, etc.)?
faq.answer: The Model requires an obvious means to move vertically.

faq.question: Does the use of an ability or battle drill that gives a Unit/Model “+X” movement increase the half movement distance a Model can make to avoid Overwatch?
faq.answer: Yes, increase the movement value before determining the half movement value.

faq.question: Can you Ace Armor Checks?
faq.answer: No, you can only Ace Skill Checks.

faq.question: Do Units with multiple primary weapons (i.e. Peacemakers or Razorbacks) get to fire all of them as a single Action or just one?
faq.answer: Non-vehicle Models can only use one weapon profile per Shoot action or Reaction, not all of their listed weapons.

faq.question: Can you Target additional Models with Auto, if said Models are within your minimum range, but within 4” of the original Target Model?
faq.answer: No, you cannot Target Models within a weapons minimum range.

faq.question: How does Seeking work with the matched play Cover rules?
faq.answer: Weapons with the Seeking special rule will only ignore the -1D10 (Shot) penalty of Models benefiting from Full Cover.

faq.question: When using a weapon that says it ignores Cover, do I roll 2D10 or 3D10 for the attack?
faq.answer: If the Target is in Cover you will roll 2D10 as you ignore the -1D10 to your Skill Check of your Target being in Cover, but you do not gain the +1D10 of your Target being in the open.

faq.question: How does Auto interact with Cyclic?
faq.answer: When an active Model makes a Shoot action with a weapon that has both Auto and Cyclic special rules, the sequence of actions goes like;

faq.question: 1. Make the first attack for the weapon, return fire Reaction is applicable here. 2. Overwatch Reactions to the Shoot action occur. 3. The Model makes additional Auto Shoot actions, these cannot be Reacted to. 4. The Model then chooses to either gain a Ready Token, or repeat steps 1-3. UN Raid Force Alpha Specialists, if the sniper moves with the deployed weapon, do they still roll 2D10 from the faction rule?
faq.answer: No, the Deployed USR only allows from 1D10 to be rolled if the Model moved, thus stopping the Force Rule from triggering.

faq.question: What is the difference between Melee and CQC?
faq.answer: -Melee is a special rule for certain weapons, and is resolved as a modified Shoot Action. -CQC occurs after a Model has completed its action and it is still in Base-to-Base with an enemy infantry Model and is a skill check roll off [3.3]. CQC continues until one Model is destroyed.

faq.question: Does the Drone auto lose CQC or does a CQC not occur?
faq.answer: As Drones cannot participate in CQC, no CQC would occur, even if an enemy Model is in Base-to-Base contact.

faq.question: Can Models in CQC range still Shoot?
faq.answer: Yes, Models in CQC can still perform Actions and Reactions.

faq.question: When does CQC happen?
faq.answer: CQC occurs after the Model has completed its Action and after Overwatch Reactions have been resolved. So a Model can Reposition into Base-to-Base, be Reacted to, perform an Action, be Reacted to, and then resolve CQC.

faq.question: When does CQC occur, after a melee action?
faq.answer: After the Model using the melee weapon has finished its action and any Models have finished their Reactions.

faq.question: When does a CQC finish?
faq.answer: When a Model is Destroyed. If both Models survive the initial CQC, roll the Models’ Skill Checks again (keeping the Damage applied by the previous rolls) until a Model is Destroyed.

faq.question: When placing a Blast Token, do I have to see the center of the Token, or do I only need to see part of it?
faq.answer: You only need to be able to draw line of sight to part of the Blast Token when placing it.

faq.question: What special rules apply to Blast attacks? AP and HE versions -For AP, the following USR’s would apply: AP, Auto, CQB, Cyclic, Deployed, EMP, Heavy, Indirect, Medium, Seeking, Sustained. -For HE, the following USR’s would apply: Cyclic, Deployed, EMP, Heavy, Medium. Do keywords that provide ‘+1 Shot’ apply to Blast rolls when using them against Infantry?
faq.answer: Eg. Heavy Yes.

faq.question: Do Blast Markers require Line of Sight to the Models within their Blast radius?
faq.answer: No, Blast affects all Models within X” of it, regardless of Line of Sight or Cover. Always agree with your opponent on if this is realistic.

faq.question: What is the Damage of Blast Weapons?
faq.answer: When Targeting Dusters or Vehicles, use the Damage number printed on the card for the Weapon. When using Blast [X], it is essentially Damage 1 with the Target rolling 1D10 for each success of the Skill Check.

faq.question: Can I place a Blast Marker outside of Line of Sight?
faq.answer: Only if your opponent agrees. We like to eyeball it and say if it’s a thrown weapon like a hand grenade, then you can simply halve the Range of the Weapon to simulate the arc.

faq.question: When you roll the same amount of successes when making a Return Fire Reaction, who wins?
faq.answer: Whichever Model rolled the highest result on a D10 in their Skill Check. If both Models tie, reroll the Skill Check and any Special Rules.

faq.question: How does Indirect work with the Blast special rule?
faq.answer: When Targeting Vehicles and Dusters, the Unit Shooting the weapon may use another Model’s line of sight.

faq.question: Can Blast [X] weapons be used in Reactions?
faq.answer: Only against vehicles and Dusters, as Blast Weapons cannot Target Infantry Models.

faq.question: Can Blast weapons trigger the Return Fire Reaction?
faq.answer: Yes, as using Blast weapons is still making a Shoot Action.

faq.question: Does Cyclic work on Reaction Shooting?
faq.answer: No, Cyclic only triggers on a Shoot Action, which can only be done by an active Unit.

faq.question: Do Data Attacks have a maximum range or require Line of sight?
faq.answer: If a Range is listed on the Data weapon, that is its maximum range. If no Range is listed, it has unlimited Range.

faq.question: Can Data Knives give Units a Pinned Token?
faq.answer: No, Data Knives and Data Spikes cause different results when they succeed a Data Attack Action:

faq.question: What happens when you get an Ace on the backside of a duster? Is it double Damage+1 or does it double the bonus Damage as well?
faq.answer: The weapon gaining +1 Damage occurs before dice are rolled in the Shooting action or Reaction.

faq.question: Does a Smoke Token have a certain height?
faq.answer: For blocking Line of Sight, no. For counting as in Smoke, yes. Models within 3” of the Token, measured vertically, are in Smoke. Imagine it as having infinite height for drawing Line of Sight through it, but not for counting Models as in Smoke.

faq.question: Are Models considered to be in Smoke if any part of their base is within Smoke, or if their whole base is?
faq.answer: Models are considered in Smoke if any part of their base is within the Smoke effect.

errata.target: [1.7] LINE OF SIGHT Add the following paragraph;
errata.oldTextSummary: See core rulebook section referenced by [1.7] LINE OF SIGHT Add the following paragraph;.
errata.newTextSummary: [1.7.3] Intervening Models Line of Sight cannot be made if it would pass fully through an infantry Model, if the Target is also an infantry Model.

citations.label: RULEBOOK - [1.7] LINE OF SIGHT Add the following paragraph;

errata.target: [1.9] FORCE DOCUMENT Add the following paragraph;
errata.oldTextSummary: See core rulebook section referenced by [1.9] FORCE DOCUMENT Add the following paragraph;.

citations.label: RULEBOOK - [1.9] FORCE DOCUMENT Add the following paragraph;

errata.target: AP - Targeting Dusters and Vehicles (As per normal Shooting rules).
errata.newTextSummary: • Pick a Duster or Vehicle Model that your Shooting Model has line of sight to, and is within the Weapon’s Range. • Determine Cover. • Make Skill Check (2D10), +1D10 if the Target is not in Cover or Data Attacks don’t require Line of Sight to their Target. -1D10 if they are.

citations.label: AP - Targeting Dusters and Vehicles (As per normal Shooting rules).

errata.newTextSummary: • Place a 25mm Blast Marker within line of sight and range of the Shooting Model. • This Marker must be flat and stable on the table or terrain. Eg. Shouldn’t be overhanging off an edge, or floating, nor overlapping a model’s base. • The Shooting Model makes a Skill Check (2D10), and may be modified by special rules. Determine successes. • Infantry Models within X” of the Blast Marker must make an Armor Check, with the number of D10’s rolled being equal to the number of successes in the Shooting Models Skill Check. • For every failed Armor Check roll, the Model takes 1 Damage. • Using this mode does not count as Targeting a Model for the purposes of interacting with Special Rules, eg; Indirect, Seeking etc.

errata.target: Boone Recon Force - Small Unit Tactics Your Models get to Shoot during the reposition step instead of moving, then you don’t get to take an action or movement in the action step with those Models.
errata.oldTextSummary: Dusters You will require the Impact Expansion to find the core Front Line Duster Rules.
errata.newTextSummary: Dusters You will require the Impact Expansion to find the core Front Line Duster Rules.

citations.label: Boone Recon Force - Small Unit Tactics Your Models get to Shoot during the reposition step instead of moving, then you don’t get to take an action or movement in the action step with those Models.

errata.target: UN Raid Force Alpha and Black Pact - Close Assault Replace with the following paragraph;
errata.oldTextSummary: Dusters You will require the Impact Expansion to find the core Front Line Duster Rules.
errata.newTextSummary: Models in this Unit may Move 2” after completing their Action, only if this Move would bring them into CQC with an Enemy Model.

citations.label: UN Raid Force Alpha and Black Pact - Close Assault Replace with the following paragraph;

errata.target: UN 3rd Battalion - Switchback You cannot use the Jump Special Rule during the Pre-Activation 2” Move.
errata.oldTextSummary: Dusters You will require the Impact Expansion to find the core Front Line Duster Rules.
errata.newTextSummary: • Dusters do not have to spend Movement to rotate, and they do not need to Move in straight lines.

citations.label: UN 3rd Battalion - Switchback You cannot use the Jump Special Rule during the Pre-Activation 2” Move.

errata.target: Impisi Insurgents - Force Rule Replace with the following paragraph;
errata.oldTextSummary: Dusters You will require the Impact Expansion to find the core Front Line Duster Rules.
errata.newTextSummary: • Dusters do not provide Cover to Models behind them. • The first paragraph of the Front Line Duster Combat rules replaces the core rule: RUN THEM OVER!. • Dusters may only fire 1 weapon listed on their profile when making a Shoot Action and Overwatch and Return Fire Reactions. • Enemy Models may choose to Target the Dusters Mobility or Chassis. - Models must be able to see the Duster’s entire base to Target its Mobility. Destroying the Duster if its Mobility Damage Track is filled. - When determining if a Model can see the Duster’s entire base, if that Model’s line of sight must pass through Smoke to see, the Model cannot Target the Duster’s Mobility. Note: This is if the Smoke is between the two Models, if one of the Models are in range of the Smoke Token, then the Attacker only has their dice modified, but can still Target the Mobility of the Duster. When using a Combat Load weapon, the Duster will use the Combat Load Weapon profile in its Shooting Action, instead of the Weapon Profile listed on its card. After completing an Activation with any Impisi Insurgent Unit, spend one Control Point to return a Grunt Model to the Unit. Place the Model within 2” of another Model in the Unit (not in Line of Sight or within 8” of an enemy). You may never exceed the number of Grunts on the Unit Card. Note: This rule applies to any Unit in the Impisi Insurgents Force, not only the Impisi Insurgents Unit Card.

citations.label: Impisi Insurgents - Force Rule Replace with the following paragraph;

universalSpecialRules.name: AI
universalSpecialRules.currentText: This Model may be affected by Special Rules that only affect AI Models.

universalSpecialRules.name: AP (X)
universalSpecialRules.currentText: When a weapon with AP(X) inflicts damage, increase the Target’s Armor Check by +X for that attack. This makes their armor less effective—they must roll higher numbers to block damage.

notes.name: Example: A Model with Armor 2/4 hit by an AP(2) Weapon would now have Armor 2/6 for that attack
notes.text: Example: A Model with Armor 2/4 hit by an AP(2) Weapon would now have Armor 2/6 for that attack

citations.label: USR AP (X) Example: A Model with Armor 2/4 hit by an AP(2) Weapon would now have Armor 2/6 for that attack

universalSpecialRules.name: Auto (X)
universalSpecialRules.currentText: After making a Shoot Action with this Weapon, this Model may make up to X additional Shoot Actions with that Weapon, that cannot be Reacted to. Each additional attack must target a different Model within 4” of the original Target. Synergies: If the Weapon also has the Cyclic special rule, the Model may chain multiple Auto(X) attacks, benefiting from the extra Shoot Action granted by Cyclic. Limitations: The original Target and all additional Targets must still fall within the Weapon’s minimum and maximum Range.

universalSpecialRules.name: Controller
universalSpecialRules.currentText: This Unit may activate a friendly Unit after completing its Activation, without spending a Control Point. If Controller is granted by a Specialist, the ability is lost when that Model is removed from play.

universalSpecialRules.name: CQB
universalSpecialRules.currentText: This Weapon may re-roll all failed Shots against a Target within half of its maximum range.

notes.name: Example: A Weapon with a 24″ Range may re-roll failed Shots against a Target within 12″
notes.text: Example: A Weapon with a 24″ Range may re-roll failed Shots against a Target within 12″

citations.label: USR CQB Example: A Weapon with a 24″ Range may re-roll failed Shots against a Target within 12″

universalSpecialRules.name: Cyclic
universalSpecialRules.currentText: After making a Shoot Action with this Weapon, this Model may immediately make one additional Shoot or Ready Action. The extra Shoot Action is treated the same as any regular Shoot Action, subject to Weapon's special rules. Limitations: Additional Shoot Action must be made with the Weapon that triggered Cyclic. Cyclic cannot be used in Reactions.

universalSpecialRules.name: Data Knife
universalSpecialRules.currentText: This Model may make a Data Attack Action targeting an AI or Powered Model within Range. On a successful Skill Check, deal 1 Damage with no Armor Check allowed.

universalSpecialRules.name: Data Spike
universalSpecialRules.currentText: This Model may make a Data Attack Action to either apply a Pinned Marker to an enemy Unit or remove a Pinned Marker from a friendly Unit, on a successful Skill Check.

universalSpecialRules.name: Deployed
universalSpecialRules.currentText: If this Model Moved during its Reposition Step, it rolls only 1D10 when using this Weapon in its Activation. Reactions are not affected.

universalSpecialRules.name: Drone
universalSpecialRules.currentText: This Model cannot engage in CQC, use ladders, or Lean Out to gain Line of Sight.

notes.name: Note: Drones do not participate in CQC — if an enemy Model moves into Base-to-Base contact, no Close Quarters Combat occurs
notes.text: Note: Drones do not participate in CQC — if an enemy Model moves into Base-to-Base contact, no Close Quarters Combat occurs

citations.label: USR Drone Note: Drones do not participate in CQC — if an enemy Model moves into Base-to-Base contact, no Close Quarters Combat occurs

universalSpecialRules.name: EMP
universalSpecialRules.currentText: This Weapon can only Damage Units with the AI Special Rule, and that Damage ignores Armor Checks.

universalSpecialRules.name: Heavy
universalSpecialRules.currentText: If this Model did not Move during its Reposition Step, it rolls an extra 1D10 when using this Weapon in its Activation. Reactions with this Weapon always roll an extra 1D10.

universalSpecialRules.name: Indirect
universalSpecialRules.currentText: This Weapon may Target an enemy Model in Line of Sight to another Model in its Group, but Range is still measured from the firing Model.

universalSpecialRules.name: Jammer
universalSpecialRules.currentText: Models within range of a Jammer ignore the effects of Pinned Markers on their Unit, though Models outside the Jammer's range remain Pinned.

universalSpecialRules.name: Lethal
universalSpecialRules.currentText: This Model rolls 3D10 for CQC Skill Checks.

universalSpecialRules.name: Low Tech
universalSpecialRules.currentText: This Unit cannot gain Pinned Markers from Data Attacks and cannot be targeted by Weapons with the Indirect special rule.

universalSpecialRules.name: Medium
universalSpecialRules.currentText: This Weapon may re-roll one failed Shot when used in a Shoot Action.

universalSpecialRules.name: Melee
universalSpecialRules.currentText: This Weapon ignores Cover penalties. When used in a Shoot Action, place the attacker Base-to-Base with its Target before resolving the Action and any Reactions. Targets may only Return Fire with a Melee Weapon or Juke.

universalSpecialRules.name: Outsourced
universalSpecialRules.currentText: Models in this Unit cannot use Battle Drills, Armory Items, or the Force Rule.

notes.name: Note: Some Specialists may have exceptions to this rule — for example, the E4 Mafia in Hired Killers may explicitly use Battle Drills
notes.text: Note: Some Specialists may have exceptions to this rule — for example, the E4 Mafia in Hired Killers may explicitly use Battle Drills

citations.label: USR Outsourced Note: Some Specialists may have exceptions to this rule — for example, the E4 Mafia in Hired Killers may explicitly use Battle Drills

universalSpecialRules.name: Powered
universalSpecialRules.currentText: This Model may be affected by Special Rules that affect Powered Models.

universalSpecialRules.name: Reinforcements
universalSpecialRules.currentText: At the end of this Unit’s Activation, mark one box to return a Destroyed Model from this Unit. Place the returned Model within 4” of another Model from this Unit and out of Line of Sight of all Enemy Models.

universalSpecialRules.name: Seeking
universalSpecialRules.currentText: This Weapon ignores the -1D10 penalty for shooting a Target in Cover.

notes.name: Example: When firing a Seeking Weapon, you roll 2D10 against Targets in Cover, and 3D10 against Targets not in Cover
notes.text: Example: When firing a Seeking Weapon, you roll 2D10 against Targets in Cover, and 3D10 against Targets not in Cover

citations.label: USR Seeking Example: When firing a Seeking Weapon, you roll 2D10 against Targets in Cover, and 3D10 against Targets not in Cover

universalSpecialRules.name: Shield
universalSpecialRules.currentText: This Model and one Friendly Infantry Model in Base-to-Base with it both gain Cover. Only one Friendly Model may be in Base-to-Base with this Model at a time.

universalSpecialRules.name: Smoke Grenade
universalSpecialRules.currentText: Before this Unit’s Reposition Phase, place a Smoke Token within X” of one of its Models. Note: See Rules [8.8] Smoke for more information about this core ability.

universalSpecialRules.name: Smoke Grenade | X"
universalSpecialRules.currentText: Before this Unit's Reposition Phase, place a Smoke Token within X” of one of its Models.

notes.name: Note: See Rules [8.8] Smoke for more information about this core ability
notes.text: Note: See Rules [8.8] Smoke for more information about this core ability

citations.label: USR Smoke Grenade | X" Note: See Rules [8.8] Smoke for more information about this core ability

universalSpecialRules.name: Spotter
universalSpecialRules.currentText: If at least two Models in this Unit can draw Line of Sight to the same Enemy Model, Models in this Unit that target that Enemy gain Medium for that Action.

universalSpecialRules.name: Sustained (X)
universalSpecialRules.currentText: When making an Armor Check against this Weapon, the Target must re-roll up to (X) successful Armor dice, continuing until that many have been re-rolled or all rolls fail.

notes.name: Example: A Model with Armor 1/6 is hit by a Sustained (2) Weapon. It rolls 1D10 for Armor. If it succeeds, it must reroll that die up to 2 times or until it fails. The final roll determines the result
notes.text: Example: A Model with Armor 1/6 is hit by a Sustained (2) Weapon. It rolls 1D10 for Armor. If it succeeds, it must reroll that die up to 2 times or until it fails. The final roll determines the result

citations.label: USR Sustained (X) Example: A Model with Armor 1/6 is hit by a Sustained (2) Weapon. It rolls 1D10 for Armor. If it succeeds, it must reroll that die up to 2 times or until it fails. The final roll determines the result

universalSpecialRules.name: Team Leader
universalSpecialRules.currentText: When this Model gains a Ready Token, it may instead give that token to another Model in the same Unit that has already made an Action this round. The receiving Model is treated as if it gained the Ready Token normally.
